LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER GREG P. DIXON'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. THE RECORD SUPPORTS THE ALJ'S FINDINGS THAT PETITIONER DID NOT COMPLETE THE BREATH TEST. THE RECORD ALSO SUPPORTS THE CONCLUSION THAT THE PETITIONER DID NOT REFUSE TO TAKE THE BLOOD TEST. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

calendar.matters:
Petitioner Greg P. Dixon'S Order To Show Cause (Re Petition For Writ Of Mandate)
Off Calendar Oct-09-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Bonnie Chen of the Department of Justice, 510-622-2113, appearing for respondent Department of Motor Vehicles
Kirk Elliott of Roberts & Elliott, LLP, 408-275-9800, appearing for petitioner Greg P. Dixon

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Petitioner Greg P. Dixon's Order to Show Cause re: Petition for Writ of Mandate is denied. The record supports the ALJ's findings that petitioner did not complete the breath test. The record also supports the conclusion that the petitioner did not refuse to take the blood test. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
